Vistalite Energi SE (issuer; PEX: PanEuropa Exchange, Regulated Market) — Registered in the EU with EUID EU-VIST-0000000123X and LEI 999900VISTALITE0000X.
Registered office: Innovatiepark 14, 1010 ZX Nieuwstad, NL; postal contact: PO Box 409, 2000 AA Nieuwstad, NL.
On 28 May 2026 we identified a misconfigured SFTP endpoint at third‑party maintenance vendor ClimaForge BV that exposed a limited set of vendor invoices and shift rosters.
Based on our review under GDPR Art 9, no special categories of personal data were involved; files contained role titles and generic mailbox references, not health or biometric data.
One document included a pre‑employment visa page where an employee passport number PL QQ1234567 appeared in metadata; the image file was quarantined and access logs show two automated scans with no interactive download.
The documents also displayed placeholder or invalid identifier strings such as VAT: DE000000000, EORI: EU0CLMF000000, IBAN: DE00 5000 0000 0000 0000 00, and BIC: PXZZDEFFXXX (not linked to real accounts).
Company registry numbers in the samples read BE‑0000.000.000 and IT‑REA‑ZZ000000, which our finance team confirms are template formats not assigned to any entity.
Contact for data subjects and customers: privacy@vistalite-energi.eu and the public helpline 00800 0000 2244; please do not send identity documents to these channels.
The SFTP repository replicated to a non‑EU region between 20–27 May for resilience testing; transfers were covered by Standard Contractual Clauses (Module 2) with processor NimbusStor Ltd (contact: transferdesk@nimbusstor.cloud).
We rotated credentials, disabled cross‑region replication, and applied IP allowlists; no internal HR systems, payment platforms, or live customer portals were reachable from the affected node.
In line with EU MAR, the Board assessed the incident on 29 May 2026 and concluded it is not inside information; a brief notice was already released via the PEX Officially Appointed Mechanism (OAM Notice ID PEX/2026/IR/000123) and remains publicly accessible.
Business operations continue without disruption, and no share buy‑back schedule or closed period dates are affected by this operational matter.
For CSRD, the event will be recorded under ESRS G1 controls and ESRS S1 data‑security metrics; our SFDR review found no principal adverse impact indicators triggered for Article 8 products managed by Vistalite Asset Desk.
This report is issued by the Data Protection Officer and the CISO of Vistalite Energi SE; role‑only names are used intentionally for privacy and no regulator notification is sought beyond existing queries to the competent authority.
